summary: Tested backend brochures endpoints (list + PDF download + 404) and previously passing endpoints. Ran Playwright tests for Home hero canvas + scroll fade, Gallery preview, Brochures section (5 cards + PDF fetch validation for NASA), and confirmed all other routes render without runtime errors. All 12 backend tests and all frontend assertions passed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py logger is defined AFTER it's used inside create_contact (line 77 references logger, but logger is instantiated at line 338). If create_contact is invoked before module fully loads it could NameError; in practice module-level import order guarantees this runs, but best practice is to define logger before routes.

reportlab and PDF generation is fully synchronous inside an async endpoint (`download_brochure`) — for higher load this will block the event loop; consider running inside `run_in_threadpool` (fastapi.concurrency).
HeroCanvas listens to window scroll but does not throttle; fine for this scale but consider requestAnimationFrame throttling for lower-end devices.
Home.jsx scroll listener also manipulates DOM style directly on every scroll without RAF batching — acceptable but combined with HeroCanvas's own scroll handler doubles the work per scroll.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: One TEST_User contact inquiry created via POST /api/contact
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Brochures IDs are day-camp, astronomy, space-comm, egypt, nasa. PDF endpoint returns ~2.6KB PDFs via reportlab.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All requested features validated successfully: (1) hero canvas + scroll fade working (opacity dropped from 1.0 to 0.177 after scrollY=800), (2) gallery preview shows 8 items with CTA to /gallery, (3) brochures section renders 5 cards with correct download hrefs and NASA PDF fetch returns application/pdf with %PDF header and 2669 bytes.
